questions.question: Your anti-skid system monitors wheel speed during landing and releases the brake if the wheel speed is below 80% of the aircraft ground speed. Your aircraft has 16" diameter tires, and the normal touchdown speed is 97 knots. Below what wheel speed will the anti-skid trigger when the aircraft is at touchdown speed?
A 48" x 40" pallet is loaded with a Packard V-1650 Merlin engine weighing in at 746 kg. How many pounds per square inch of pressure is being exerted on the pallet?

Airflow is the primary means of cooling an engine. The manufacturer has determined that decelerating from 100 KTS to 95 KTS, the engine is warmed by 7°F. However, for every 5-knot reduction in airspeed, the rate of temperature increase is reduced by 2.5%. Cruising at 90 knots the engine indicates 170°F. After slowing to 47 KTS, what is the new engine temperature?

Just before turning on to runway 15 (true heading 141°), you calculate your takeoff distance. The POH shows 981 feet for a dry runway with a note to increase 7% for every 4° Celsius above standard and decrease 4% for every 5° Celsius below standard. It then notes to increase the distance by 10% when wet. If the elevation is 7520' and the temperature is 59°F, how many meters will you travel before you lift off?

The max takeoff weight of a B737 (two-engine passenger jet) is 85,139 LBS and each engine produces 13,150 LBS of thrust. While the Challenger 300's (two-engine corporate jet) max takeoff weight is 38,850 LBS and each engine produces 6,826 LBS of thrust. How much better is the Challenger 300's thrust to weight ratio?

After performing a rejected takeoff at 98 knots, you want to know how hot your brakes are. Looking at the manufacturer testing data, you see that maximum braking at 120 knots will increase brake temperatures by 35°C per 10 knots of speed reduction. Additionally, they note that for every 10 knots of speed reduction, the rate of temperature increase is reduced by 6%. If you applied maximum braking until reaching 20 knots, what were your brake temperatures?

The manufacturer testing data notes that your airplane experiences 450 lbs. of parasite drag at a normal cruise speed of 145 knots. You remember from your aerodynamics class that parasite drag increases with the square of an increase in airspeed. If you slow down to 60 knots, how much parasite drag is the airplane experiencing?
What is the square root of 302?
You are climbing at a constant rate, at 15° nose up. If each of your two engines produces 8700 lbs. of thrust, what is the vertical component of thrust?

Your aircraft has two altimeters. On the ground, Altimeter A reads 5,360 feet, and Altimeter B reads 5,420 feet. If the difference between the altimeters increases by 10% for every 10,000 feet you climb, what will Altimeter B read if Altimeter A indicates 35,000 feet?

You and your 6 friends go out for dinner and decide to split the check equally. If the bill subtotal came to $238, the tax rate is 5.75%, and you want to tip 18% of the total bill, how much do you add to your own bill for the tip?

You are transporting a drum with 48 gallons of AVGAS to northern Alaska. The drum sits on a pallet measuring 24 inches square. Your aircraft has a floor load limit of 80 lbs/square feet. Can you load the barrel with the pallet it rests on now and remain within the limitation? (disregard the weight of the drum and pallet itself)

choices.text: No, the pallet needs to be 0.6 square feet larger
Yes, the pallet is large enough by 0.4 square feet
Yes, the pallet is large enough by 0.8 square feet
No, the pallet needs to be 8 square feet larger

questions.correctChoice: Yes, the pallet is large enough by 0.4 square feet

questions.question: If the fighter's average wind is 010@52 miles per hour and course to the intercept point is 046°, using the information from the previous question and an average cost of $13,500 per hour, what is the taxpayer expense for him to get to the intercept point?
questions.context: In the preflight briefing for your upcoming 328 statute mile cross country trip you learn that there will be a TFR directly under your true course of 332 degrees. The center of the TFR will be located 185 nautical miles along your route. The TFR is 5 nautical miles across, from the surface to 7,000 feet, and will be active from 1400Z to 1600Z. Use this information to answer the next 7 questions. [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 15: The fighter has an average speed of 582 knots and has to travel 304 nautical miles to his intercept point. He'll climb at 5,800 feet per minute from sea level to 17,000 feet. How long will that climb take?

questions.question: Find pressure altitude. Elevation 7770'. Cruising at 17,500' MSL, -22°C, Altimeter 29.29" Hg.
What is the standard temperature at 19,500'?

The floor in your cargo area is only approved for a load of 50 pounds per square foot. If you put the drum on a 25-pound pallet that is 36" square, will you even be able to take a 55-gallon drum of fuel?

choices.text: Yes, and there is a 5% safety margin.
Yes, and you have 8 pounds per square foot to spare.
No, you cannot take the fuel because the floor load is exceeded
No, and the floor load limit is exceeded by 16 pounds per square foot.

questions.correctChoice: Yes, and you have 8 pounds per square foot to spare.
questions.context: While preparing to take a load of cargo to a remote backcountry airstrip in the desert, you observe two steel 55-gallon drums. The freight forwarder has told you that one is completely filled with oil and that one is completely filled with AVGAS, but he can't remember which one is which. The following questions are based on this situation:

questions.question: If the airport elevation is 5805 feet and the altimeter setting is 30.57 inches of mercury, what is the pressure altitude?
What is the square root of 178?
What is the square root of 538? (Provide an answer to nearest 1/10)

Tom, a former Navy pilot, one day recalls his training at Cecil Field. He remembers using an airfield to the northwest that was 5300 feet long with a 750 foot section marked off for practice carrier ops. He then wonders, what percentage of the field was not used?
Using the above information, if it costs (non-fuel costs) $80 per minute to operate the G IV and $40 per minute to operate the King Air, which is cheaper to operate on their respective routes?
questions.context: You are flying for a corporation that has many corporate aircraft. On two separate days in two separate aircraft (a G IV and King Air), you are asked to fly from Madison, WI (800 feet MSL) to Rapid City, SD (2200 feet MSL). You fly in a standard atmosphere unless otherwise stated. Route 1 is with the G IV and Route 2 is with the King Air: [[TABLE]]

rows: MSN to RAP direct (602 nautical miles)
G IV burns 2000 pounds of Jet A per hour and travels at Mach .77 (-45° C)
Leg 1 (MSN to DSM), Leg 2 (DSM to RAP): 294 nautical miles and 422 nautical miles respectively
King Air burns 750 pounds of Jet A per hour and travels at 230 knots true airspeed
Des Moines, IA (600 feet MSL) is a landing airport

questions.question: Maximum operating airspeed (Vmo) for the de Havilland DHC-8-201 at 14000' is 242 kts. Maximum operating airspeed (Vmo) for the de Havilland DHC-8-201 at 15000' is 239 kts. Maximum operating airspeed (Vmo) for the de Havilland DHC-8-201 at 20000' is 223 kts. What would you expect Vmo to be at 25000'?

Flying direct from Tokyo to San Francisco at Flight Level 410, the DME shows you are at 88.5 nm from San Francisco. Directly below is a ship that is 88.0 nm from San Francisco, how many nautical miles above the earth's surface are you?
